# Processed Content ## Text clean_text: ``` # Quarterly Business Performance Report

Quarterly Business Performance Report

Q4 2025 - Acme Corporation

This report provides a comprehensive analysis of Acme Corporation's performance during Q4 2025.

Revenue grew 12% year-over-year, driven primarily by expansion in the enterprise segment. Operating margins improved to 23.5%, up from 21.2% in the prior quarter, reflecting cost optimization initiatives and favorable product mix.

Key highlights include the successful launch of the AcmeCloud Pro platform, which acquired 2,400 new enterprise accounts in its first 60 days. Customer retention remained strong at 94.2%, and Net Promoter Score reached an all-time high of 72.

Financial Summary

Metric Q3 2025 Q4 2025 YoY Change
Revenue ($M) $142.3 $159.4 +12.0%
Gross Margin 68.1% 69.8% +1.7pp
Operating Margin 21.2% 23.5% +2.3pp
Net Income ($M) $28.4 $35.2 +23.9%
Customers 12,450 14,850 +19.3%
ARR ($M) $580 $648 +11.7%

Product Performance

AcmeCloud Pro launched on October 15th and exceeded all internal targets. The platform's AI-powered analytics suite proved to be the key differentiator, with 78% of new customers citing it as their primary reason for adoption. Average contract value for Pro tier customers is $48,000/year, compared to $18,500/year for the standard tier.

The legacy AcmeDB product continues to generate stable revenue at $42M/quarter, though new customer acquisition has slowed. Migration tools released in November have begun moving AcmeDB customers to AcmeCloud, with 340 accounts transitioning during the quarter.

Regional Breakdown

Region Revenue % of Total Growth
North America $89.2M 56% +8.5%
Europe $38.1M 24% +15.2%
Asia-Pacific $22.5M 14% +22.8%
Rest of World $9.6M 6% +11.1%

Strategic Outlook - 2026

Looking ahead to 2026, Acme Corporation is well-positioned for continued growth. Key strategic priorities include:

  1. Expanding AcmeCloud Pro to the mid-market segment with a self-serve onboarding flow
  2. Launching AcmeCloud for Government with FedRAMP certification expected in Q2
  3. Deepening AI capabilities through the planned acquisition of DataSynth Labs
  4. Establishing direct sales presence in Japan and South Korea

Management guidance for FY2026 projects revenue of $700-720M (up 10-13% YoY) with operating margins expanding to 25-26%. The company plans to increase R&D investment by 18% while maintaining sales efficiency ratios above 1.2x.

``` ## Images processed_image: ``` # Concise Captions **No captions needed** - The document contains no images, figures, charts, graphs, or visual elements requiring captions. --- ## Summary - **Page 1:** Text and Financial Summary table only - **Page 2:** Text and Regional Breakdown table only - **Page 3:** Text only All content is textual/tabular with no visual elements to caption. ```
